root_text.an7.95:1.1: “Sattime, bhikkhave, puggalā āhuneyyā …pe… dakkhiṇeyyā añjalikaraṇīyā anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.95:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo cakkhusmiṁ aniccānupassī viharati aniccasaññī aniccapaṭisaṁvedī satataṁ samitaṁ abbokiṇṇaṁ cetasā adhimuccamāno paññāya pariyogāhamāno.
root_text.an7.95:1.4: So āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an7.95:1.5: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, paṭhamo puggalo āhuneyyo pāhuneyyo …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.95:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo cakkhusmiṁ aniccānupassī viharati aniccasaññī aniccapaṭisaṁvedī satataṁ samitaṁ abbokiṇṇaṁ cetasā adhimuccamāno paññāya pariyogāhamāno.
root_text.an7.95:2.2: Tassa apubbaṁ acarimaṁ āsavapariyādānañca hoti jīvitapariyādānañca.
root_text.an7.95:2.3: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyo puggalo āhuneyyo …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.95:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo cakkhusmiṁ aniccānupassī viharati aniccasaññī aniccapaṭisaṁvedī satataṁ samitaṁ abbokiṇṇaṁ cetasā adhimuccamāno paññāya pariyogāhamāno.
root_text.an7.95:3.2: So pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti …pe…
root_text.an7.95:3.7: Ayaṁ, bhikkhave, sattamo puggalo āhuneyyo …pe… anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an7.95:3.8: Ime kho, bhikkhave, satta puggalā āhuneyyā pāhuneyyā dakkhiṇeyyā añjalikaraṇīyā anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā”ti.

translation_text.an7.95:1.1: “Mendicants, these seven individuals are worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of greeting with joined palms, and are the supreme field of merit for the world.
translation_text.an7.95:1.3: First, take an individual who meditates observing impermanence in the eye. They perceive impermanence and experience impermanence. Constantly, continually, and without interruption, they apply the mind and fathom with wisdom.
translation_text.an7.95:1.4: They realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life. And they live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an7.95:1.5: This is the first individual who is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of greeting with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.
translation_text.an7.95:2.1: Next, take an individual who meditates observing impermanence in the eye. …
translation_text.an7.95:2.2: Their defilements and their life come to an end at exactly the same time.
translation_text.an7.95:2.3: This is the second individual who is worthy of offerings …
translation_text.an7.95:3.1: Next, take an individual who meditates observing impermanence in the eye. …
translation_text.an7.95:3.2: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished between one life and the next. …
translation_text.an7.95:3.3: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished upon landing. …
translation_text.an7.95:3.4: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished without extra effort. …
translation_text.an7.95:3.5: With the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished with extra effort. …
translation_text.an7.95:3.6: With the ending of the five lower fetters they head upstream, going to the Akaniṭṭha realm. …
translation_text.an7.95:3.8: These are the seven individuals who are worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of greeting with joined palms, and are the supreme field of merit for the world.”
